title: Should I Rebrand or Reposition? | Daniel Fox
meta_description: Most rebrands are an expensive way to avoid a sentence-level fix. The adjacent market usually needs a reframe, not a new identity. Run the cheap experiment first. Daniel Fox, fractional CMO.

acceptedAnswer.text: Most rebrands are an expensive way to avoid a sentence-level fix. The adjacent market you are chasing usually needs a reframe, not a new identity: the same product and the same proof, with different facts foregrounded and a different fear addressed. Run the cheap experiment first, one campaign and one landing page reframed only, before anyone commissions identity work. Daniel Fox is a fractional CMO who runs that reframe before the spend.
